title: Fresh slick final-stop window
summary: Add the one-lap entry-into-final-stop-window features as a small local patch on 001214.
rationale: A pit stop becomes strategically viable when a fresh slick compound can newly reach the finish. This patch encodes that boundary crossing directly as local, target-free features.
implementation_hint: Start from 001214 exactly. Add only the fresh-slick final-window block using the formulas below: compute `remaining_after_1 = (remaining_laps_est - 1).clip(lower=0)`, `soft_margin_now = 17 - remaining_laps_est`, `medium_margin_now = 24 - remaining_laps_est`, `hard_margin_now = 31 - remaining_laps_est`, `soft_margin_next = 17 - remaining_after_1`, `medium_margin_next = 24 - remaining_after_1`, `hard_margin_next = 31 - remaining_after_1`. With `dry_mask = Compound in {SOFT, MEDIUM, HARD} and not testing`, define `fresh_soft_newly_viable_next_lap = (soft_margin_now < 0) & (soft_margin_next >= 0) & dry_mask`, same for medium and hard, `fresh_slick_newly_viable_next_lap_any = any of the three`, `fresh_slick_newly_viable_next_lap_count = soft + medium + hard`, `softest_newly_viable_slick = select 1 for soft, 2 for medium, 3 for hard, 0 otherwise`, and `fresh_slick_nearest_final_window_margin = min(abs(soft_margin_now), abs(medium_margin_now), abs(hard_margin_now))` or the equivalent boundary-margin logic described below. Do not add broader finishability, deployment-likeness, or service-window features in this patch.
expected_effect: Capture the discrete strategic transition where pitting now or next lap first becomes viable for a final dry stint.
risk: The fixed 17/24/31 lap templates may be too crude or already subsumed by the current multi-seed RealMLP core.
